data.title: Ready Capital Corp SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Ready Capital Corp, a prominent player in the financial services industry specializing in various loan products, has released its latest Form 10-Q report. The report highlights significant improvements in both financial and operational metrics, underscoring the company's robust performance and strategic growth initiatives.

Financial Highlights

Ready Capital Corp has demonstrated strong financial performance in its latest quarter, as evidenced by the following key metrics:

  • Interest Income: $154.967 million, reflecting a decrease from the previous year due to changes in the interest rate environment.
  • Net Interest Income After Recovery of Loan Losses: $124.069 million, significantly higher than the previous year, driven by a substantial recovery of loan losses.
  • Total Non-Interest Income (Expense): $25.736 million, showing a positive turnaround from a loss in the previous year, primarily due to a gain on bargain purchase.
  • Total Non-Interest Expense: $72.602 million, a slight decrease from the previous year, indicating effective cost management.
  • Net Income (Loss) from Continuing Operations: $82.410 million, a significant improvement from a loss in the previous year, highlighting strong operational performance.
  • Net Income (Loss): $81.965 million, marking a substantial recovery from a loss in the previous year.
  • Net Income (Loss) Attributable to Ready Capital Corporation: $77.506 million, reflecting a strong financial performance compared to a loss in the previous year.
  • Earnings Per Common Share - Basic: $0.47, indicating a positive earnings per share compared to a loss in the previous year.
  • Earnings Per Common Share - Diluted: $0.46, showing a positive earnings per share compared to a loss in the previous year.

Business Highlights

Ready Capital Corp's operational performance has been equally impressive, with several key developments and strategic initiatives:

  • Revenue Segments: The company operates in multiple segments, including LMM loans, SBA loans, construction loans, USDA loans, and mortgage-backed securities. Each segment contributes to the overall revenue, with LMM loans being a significant focus due to their unique characteristics combining commercial and residential loan features.
  • Geographical Performance: The company's loan portfolio is geographically diverse, with significant concentrations in Texas (22.4%), California (11.1%), and Florida (8.5%). This distribution reflects the company's strategic focus on regions with robust real estate markets.
  • Sales Units: The company reported a total loan portfolio of $8,383,218 thousand as of March 31, 2025, with bridge loans and SBA loans being the largest components. The increase in loan volumes indicates strong demand across various loan products.
  • New Product Launches: The company completed the acquisition of United Development Funding IV, which expands its product offerings to include capital solutions for residential real estate developers and regional homebuilders.
  • New Production Launches: The acquisition of Madison One Capital enhances the company's capabilities in originating and servicing USDA and SBA guaranteed loan products, indicating an expansion in production capacity in these areas.
  • Future Outlook: Management anticipates continued growth in the LMM loan segment, driven by favorable market conditions and strategic acquisitions. The integration of recent acquisitions is expected to enhance operational efficiencies and expand the company's market presence.

SEC Filing: Ready Capital Corp [ RC ] - 10-Q - May. 09, 2025

data.title: REDWOOD TRUST INC SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Redwood Trust Inc., a prominent player in the real estate investment trust (REIT) sector, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial and operational performance, reflecting its strategic focus on housing credit investments and mortgage banking activities.

Financial Highlights

Redwood Trust Inc. reported notable financial metrics for the third quarter:

  • Total Interest Income: $272.1 million, a significant increase from $204.7 million in the prior year period, driven by higher interest income from residential consumer loans.
  • Net Interest Income: $27.9 million, up from $24.2 million, indicating improved interest income despite rising interest expenses.
  • Total Non-Interest Income, Net: $45.9 million, slightly down from $50.3 million, with notable contributions from mortgage banking activities.
  • Net Income: $16.1 million, a decrease from $30.3 million in the previous year, impacted by higher provision for income taxes.
  • Net Income Available To Common Stockholders: $14.4 million, compared to $28.5 million, reflecting the overall decrease in net income.
  • Basic Earnings Per Common Share: $0.10, down from $0.21, consistent with the decline in net income.
  • Diluted Earnings Per Common Share: $0.10, down from $0.21, mirroring the basic earnings per share.

Business Highlights

Redwood Trust Inc. operates through three primary segments, each contributing uniquely to the company's overall performance:

  • Segment Performance: The company operates in three segments: Sequoia Mortgage Banking, CoreVest Mortgage Banking, and Redwood Investments. Each segment contributes differently to the company's overall performance, with Sequoia and CoreVest focusing on mortgage banking activities and Redwood Investments handling investment portfolios.
  • Sequoia Mortgage Banking: This segment reported significant activity in mortgage banking, with a focus on residential consumer loans. The segment experienced changes in fair value of residential consumer loans and trading securities, contributing to its net income.
  • CoreVest Mortgage Banking: CoreVest focuses on residential investor loans, including term and bridge loans. The segment saw changes in fair value of residential investor loans and risk management derivatives, impacting its financial results.
  • Redwood Investments: This segment manages the company's investment portfolio, including residential consumer and investor loans. It reported investment fair value changes and servicing income, contributing to its overall performance.
  • Residential Consumer Loans: The company holds a significant portfolio of residential consumer loans, both securitized and unsecuritized. The fair value of these loans increased during the reporting period, reflecting market conditions and loan performance.
  • Residential Investor Loans: Redwood Trust's portfolio includes residential investor term and bridge loans. The company actively manages these loans, including sales to joint ventures and securitizations, to optimize returns.
  • Home Equity Investments (HEI): The company invests in HEI contracts, which provide exposure to home price appreciation and depreciation. Redwood Trust co-sponsored HEI securitization entities, contributing to its income through market valuation gains.
  • Servicing Investments: Redwood Trust holds servicing investments, including servicer advance investments and mortgage servicing rights (MSRs). These investments generate income through interest and fair value changes.
  • Strategic Investments: The company maintains strategic investments, including joint ventures and equity method investments. These investments provide additional income streams and strategic opportunities.
  • Future Outlook: Redwood Trust aims to continue its focus on housing credit investments, leveraging its securitization platforms and investment activities to deliver attractive returns to shareholders. The company is committed to technological innovation and risk management to support its growth objectives.

SEC Filing: REDWOOD TRUST INC [ RWT ] - 10-Q - May. 09, 2025

data.title: PLAYSTUDIOS, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
PLAYSTUDIOS, Inc., a leading developer of free-to-play casual games, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter, detailing significant financial and operational metrics. The report highlights a challenging period for the company, marked by declines in revenue and user engagement, as well as increased losses. Despite these setbacks, PLAYSTUDIOS remains committed to investing in its player engagement programs to drive future growth.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Revenue: $62.7 million, a decrease of 19.4% compared to the same period last year due to a decline in virtual currency and advertising revenue.
  • Operating Loss: $2.7 million, an increase of 61.0% from the previous year, reflecting higher restructuring expenses and a decrease in revenue.
  • Net Loss: $2.9 million, a significant increase from a net loss of $0.6 million in the prior year, primarily due to decreased revenue and increased restructuring expenses.
  • Net Loss Per Share - Basic: $(0.02), compared to $0.00 in the previous year, reflecting the increased net loss.
  • Net Loss Per Share - Diluted: $(0.02), compared to $0.00 in the previous year, consistent with the basic net loss per share.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The playGAMES segment reported a decrease in net revenue by 19.6% to $62.6 million, primarily due to a decline in virtual currency and advertising revenue. The playAWARDS segment reported a net revenue of $154,000, marking its contribution to the overall revenue.
  • Geographical Performance: Revenue from the United States decreased by 18.8% to $53.1 million, while revenue from all other countries decreased by 22.6% to $9.6 million, indicating a significant decline in both domestic and international markets.
  • Sales Units: The playGAMES division experienced a decrease in Average Daily Active Users (DAU) by 24.7% to 2.6 million and Average Monthly Active Users (MAU) by 22.6% to 11.4 million, reflecting a reduction in user engagement.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to continue investing in its playAWARDS and myVIP programs to drive player engagement and retention, despite the current decline in user metrics and revenue.

SEC Filing: PLAYSTUDIOS, Inc. [ MYPS ] - 10-Q - May. 09, 2025

data.title: Identiv, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Identiv, Inc., a global leader in digital identification and security solutions, has released its Form 10-Q report for the first quarter of 2025. The report outlines significant financial and operational challenges faced by the company, including a notable decline in revenue and ongoing production transitions.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Revenue: $5.3 million, a decrease of 21% compared to the same period in 2024, primarily due to lower unit sales of RFID transponder products as the company exits low-margin business opportunities.
  • Gross Profit: $0.1 million, a 73% decrease from the comparable period in 2024, mainly due to incremental costs related to the transition of production to the Thailand facility and underutilization of manufacturing production facilities.
  • Loss from Continuing Operations: $(5.5) million, reflecting ongoing challenges in transitioning production and exiting low-margin business.
  • Net Loss: $(4.8) million, compared to $(4.6) million in the same period in 2024, with no income from discontinued operations in 2025.
  • Basic and Diluted Net Loss Per Common Share: $(0.21), consistent with the same period in 2024, reflecting the impact of lower revenue and gross profit.

Business Highlights

  • Geographical Performance: The company reported a decrease in net revenue across all regions for the three months ended March 31, 2025, compared to the same period in 2024. The Americas saw a 24% decline, Europe and the Middle East experienced a 30% decrease, while Asia-Pacific showed a 7% increase.
  • Sales Units: There was a significant reduction in unit sales of RFID transponder products as the company exited low-margin business opportunities, impacting overall sales volumes.
  • Production Transition: The company is in the process of transitioning its RFID device production from Singapore to Thailand, with the majority of production already moved in 2024. The transition is expected to be completed by the third quarter of 2025.
  • Operational Challenges: The company faced incremental costs related to the transition of production to Thailand and dual manufacturing sites, which, along with underutilization of production facilities, affected gross margins.
  • Future Outlook: The company anticipates an increase in gross product margins after completing the transition of RFID transponder production to Thailand. However, the company expects to continue incurring losses until it can substantially increase revenue to achieve scale.
  • Competitive Landscape: The company noted increased global production capacity among RFID competitors, leading to competitive pricing pressures and a strategic exit from low-margin business, which has negatively impacted operating results.

SEC Filing: Identiv, Inc. [ INVE ] - 10-Q - May. 09, 2025

data.title: Health Catalyst, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Health Catalyst, Inc., a leading provider of data and analytics technology and services to healthcare organizations, has released its Form 10-Q report for the first quarter of 2025. The report highlights the company's financial performance and key business developments, reflecting its ongoing efforts to enhance its technology and professional services offerings.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenue: $79.4 million, reflecting an increase from $74.7 million in the prior year period, driven by growth in both technology and professional services revenue.

Loss from Operations: $(20.2) million, an improvement from $(22.8) million in the prior year period, indicating better cost management.

Net Loss: $(23.7) million, compared to $(20.6) million in the prior year period, impacted by increased interest expenses.

Net Loss Per Share, Basic and Diluted: $(0.35), consistent with the prior year period, despite an increase in weighted-average shares outstanding.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: Health Catalyst operates in two primary segments: Technology and Professional Services. The Technology segment includes subscription fees for access to the company's cloud-based data platform and software analytics applications. The Professional Services segment encompasses data and analytics services, domain expertise services, Tech-enabled Managed Services, and implementation services.

Geographical Performance: The majority of Health Catalyst's revenue is derived from clients located in the United States, accounting for 96.1% of total revenue for the three months ended March 31, 2025.

New Product Launches: Health Catalyst acquired Upfront Healthcare Services, Inc., a next-generation patient engagement platform provider, which is expected to enhance the company's product offerings in patient engagement solutions.

Future Outlook: Health Catalyst continues to focus on expanding its technology and professional services offerings, with an emphasis on integrating recent acquisitions to drive operational improvements and client satisfaction. The company is also exploring opportunities for inorganic growth through strategic acquisitions.

data.title: FIRST BUSEY CORP /NV/ SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
First Busey Corporation, a diversified financial services company, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance and operational highlights, reflecting significant developments and strategic initiatives undertaken during the period.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Interest Income: $166.8 million, reflecting a significant increase from the previous year due to higher interest and fees on loans.
  • Net Interest Income: $103.7 million, an increase driven by higher interest income despite a rise in interest expenses.
  • Provision for Credit Losses: $42.5 million, a substantial increase compared to the previous year, primarily due to the establishment of an ACL on acquired loans.
  • Total Noninterest Income: $21.2 million, a decrease from the previous year, impacted by realized net losses on securities.
  • Total Noninterest Expense: $115.2 million, an increase due to higher salaries, wages, and employee benefits, as well as professional fees.
  • Net Income (Loss): $(30.0) million, a decline from the previous year, influenced by increased provision for credit losses and noninterest expenses.
  • Basic Earnings (Loss) Per Common Share: $(0.44), reflecting the net loss for the period.
  • Diluted Earnings (Loss) Per Common Share: $(0.44), consistent with the basic earnings per share due to the net loss.

Business Highlights

  • Segment Performance: First Busey Corporation operates in three segments: Banking, Wealth Management, and FirsTech. The Banking segment provides a full range of banking services across multiple states, including Arizona, Colorado, Florida, Illinois, Indiana, Kansas, Missouri, New Mexico, Oklahoma, and Texas. The Wealth Management segment offers asset management, investment, brokerage, fiduciary, philanthropic advisory, tax preparation, and farm management services. The FirsTech segment provides payment technology solutions, including online, mobile, and voice-recognition bill payments.
  • Geographical Expansion: The acquisition of CrossFirst Bank has expanded First Busey's presence in the Midwest, Southwest, and Florida, with 78 full-service locations across 10 states. This strategic move is expected to enhance the company's market reach and operational capabilities in these regions.
  • Operational Integration: Following the acquisition of CrossFirst Bank, its results of operations have been included in First Busey's consolidated results beginning March 1, 2025. The integration process is ongoing, with CrossFirst Bank expected to merge with Busey Bank by June 20, 2025, further consolidating operations.
  • Future Outlook: First Busey Corporation plans to continue operating CrossFirst Bank as a separate banking subsidiary until the merger with Busey Bank. This strategic integration is anticipated to create synergies and enhance service capabilities in the newly acquired markets.
  • Lease Commitments: The company has operating leases primarily for equipment and real estate, with lease terms extending through 2042. The acquisition of CrossFirst Bank added significant right of use assets, indicating an expansion in operational facilities.
  • Loan Portfolio Growth: The acquisition of CrossFirst Bank has significantly increased First Busey's loan portfolio, with notable growth in commercial loans, including C&I, CRE, and real estate construction loans. This expansion is expected to enhance the company's lending capabilities and market presence.
  • Risk Management: First Busey Corporation utilizes a comprehensive loan grading system to manage credit risk, with regular reviews of commercial loans to ensure credit quality. This system is crucial for maintaining the health of the loan portfolio amid expansion.
  • Commitment to Innovation: The FirsTech segment continues to provide innovative payment technology solutions, supporting the company's commitment to offering comprehensive financial services and enhancing customer experience.

SEC Filing: FIRST BUSEY CORP /NV/ [ BUSE ] - 10-Q - May. 09, 2025

data.title: ACACIA RESEARCH CORP SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Acacia Research Corporation, a company specializing in patent licensing and enforcement, as well as diversified industrial and energy operations, has released its latest Form 10-Q report. The report highlights significant improvements in both financial and operational performance, reflecting the company's strategic initiatives and robust market presence.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenues: $124.4 million, reflecting a significant increase from the previous year's $24.3 million, driven by strong performance across all operations.

Operating Income: $38.3 million, compared to an operating loss of $2.1 million in the prior year, indicating improved operational efficiency and cost management.

Net Income: $24.3 million, a substantial turnaround from a net loss of $0.2 million in the previous year, highlighting effective cost control and revenue growth.

Basic Net Income Per Common Share: $0.25, demonstrating a positive earnings per share compared to no earnings per share in the prior year.

Diluted Net Income Per Common Share: $0.25, consistent with basic earnings per share, indicating no significant dilution effects.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The company reported revenue from four main operational segments: Intellectual Property Operations, Industrial Operations, Energy Operations, and Manufacturing Operations. Intellectual Property Operations generated $69.9 million, Industrial Operations contributed $7.7 million, Energy Operations brought in $18.3 million, and Manufacturing Operations added $28.5 million.

Intellectual Property Operations: This segment focuses on patent licensing and enforcement, with revenues primarily from paid-up license agreements and recurring license revenue agreements. The segment saw a significant increase in revenue compared to the previous year.

Industrial Operations: Printronix, a key player in this segment, manufactures and distributes industrial impact printers. The segment serves diverse sectors including healthcare, food and beverage, and logistics, with a focus on cost reduction and efficiency improvements.

Energy Operations: Benchmark Energy II, LLC, in which the company holds a 73.5% interest, focuses on oil and gas production in Texas and Oklahoma. The segment aims to enhance value through field optimization and strategic acquisitions.

Manufacturing Operations: Deflecto, acquired in October 2024, is a leading manufacturer in the transportation, HVAC, and office markets. The segment benefits from long-term relationships with over 1,500 customers and operates nine manufacturing facilities globally.

Future Outlook: The company plans to continue leveraging its strategic relationship with Starboard Value, LP to identify and evaluate acquisition opportunities. The focus remains on acquiring businesses with strong cash flow generation and scalability potential.

Operational Strategy: Acacia Research Corporation emphasizes a disciplined, value-oriented approach to acquiring and operating businesses across various sectors. The company aims to create long-term value through strategic acquisitions and operational improvements.

SEC Filing: ACACIA RESEARCH CORP [ ACTG ] - 10-Q - May. 09, 2025

data.title: Latam FX mixed amid caution ahead of Sino-US trade talks
data.description:
Key points:
  • LATAM stocks up 0.6%, FX up 0.3%
  • Peru's central bank cuts rates by 25 bps
  • Brazil's annual inflation rises to 5.53% in April

Latin American currencies were mixed on Friday, and were set to end higher in a week marked by rate decisions and economic data, with investors now watching the Sino-U.S. trade talks over the weekend.

MSCI's gauge for Latin American currencies (.MILA00000CUS) nudged up 0.3% on the day, and was on track for its fourth-straight weekly gain.

U.S. President Donald Trump said an 80% tariff on Chinese goods "seems right", suggesting an alternative to his 145% levies on Chinese goods.

All eyes were now on trade negotiations between the United States and China that could potentially de-escalate the market-rattling trade war between the world's top two economies.

"Talks expected to happen over the weekend are a positive... But until clearer signs are visible, cautiousness from the side of markets will continue to be the base case," said Wilson Ferrarezi, an economist at TS Lombard.

In Brazil, the real steadied 0.2% against the dollar and was on pace to end the week with modest gains, primarily benefiting from its central bank's move on Wednesday to hike interest rates.

The central bank also left future steps open amid global uncertainties and sticky domestic inflation. On Friday, data showed April's annual inflation rate escalated to 5.53%, well above the central bank's 3% target.

Brazil's rate decision came hours after the U.S. Federal Reserve stood pat on its key benchmark rate.

Meanwhile, the country's stock index rose 0.35%, and was set for its fourth weekly gain in a row. A gauge for Latin American stocks (.MILA00000PUS) was up 1.5% for the week and rose 0.6% on Friday.

Mexico's benchmark stock index fell 0.7%, the worst performing among the local peers for the day, after it hit a one-year high in the previous session. Mexico's peso strengthened 0.3%.

The Bank of Mexico will announce its rate decision next week, with the central bank expected to keep cutting rates given the country's annual inflation rate well within its target.

Data revealed a dip in the country's consumer confidence index. The country's finance minister said Mexico's growth projection was biased to the downside.

In Peru, stocks jumped 1% after the central bank cut interest rate in an overnight surprise move after the annual inflation ticked up last month.

Colombia's peso surged 0.8%. A Reuters poll showed the country is forecast to have grown 2.6% in the first quarter of 2025, indicating expanding economic activity despite tight interest rates.

Elsewhere, Romania's leu saw its biggest weekly decline since January 2009 as political crisis deepened after hard-right presidential candidate George Simion's victory in a first-round vote on Sunday.

The International Monetary Fund said its executive board approved the first review of its $7 billion program with Pakistan, allowing the country to draw about $1 billion in cash.

data.title: Magic Empire Global to Sell Up to 15M Shrs
Health Care Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering the Health Care sector. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1044 ET - Big pharmaceutical companies' plans to expand their manufacturing footprints in the U.S. will take years to materialize, the CFO of Swiss contract drug manufacturer Lonza, Philippe Deecke, says in a call with analysts. Unless companies are sitting on land with permits, they will need to apply for them, design the sites and build them, Deecke says. "If the capacity is actually being constructed in the U.S., this will take five to ten years to materialize," he says. Big pharma groups such as Johnson & Johnson, Eli Lilly, Novartis or Roche in recent weeks outlined plans to make multibillion-dollar investments in the U.S. Lonza shares rise 2.7%. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

1041 ET - It is unclear whether big pharmaceutical companies' announcements about major investments in the U.S. will actually result in extra spending that wasn't previously planned, the CFO of Swiss contract drug manufacturer Lonza, Philippe Deecke, says. "Our understanding is that most of these investments are not necessarily additional but have been announced in one go. Most of these investments over time would probably have happened," Deecke says in a call with analysts. "Time will tell," he says. Pharma companies have provided little detail on how their investments will be allocated between research-and-development activities and manufacturing, Deecke says. Big pharma groups such as Johnson & Johnson, Eli Lilly, Novartis or Roche in recent weeks outlined plans to make multibillion-dollar investments in the U.S. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0231 ET - Riverstone Holdings' earnings are likely to fall in 2025, CGS International's William Tng says in a research report. As the Singapore-listed company sells its gloves in U.S. dollars, further weakening of the greenback will be a negative, the analyst says. Also, there's probably greater competition for market share among glove manufacturers this year, which could lead to margin pressure as the industry absorbs some of the foreign-exchange headwinds and raw-material price increases, the analyst says. CGS International cuts its 2025-2027 revenue forecasts for Riverstone by 8%-16%. It lowers the stock's rating to hold from add, and cuts its target price to S$0.84 from S$1.10. Shares are down 11% at S$0.765. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0211 ET - Bangkok Chain Hospital's 1Q net profit is likely to remain weak, Maybank Securities (Thailand)'s Nontapat Sahakitpinyo says in a research report. Factors are soft revenue growth and narrower gross profit margin from a lower mix of high-margin Kuwaiti patients as well as higher selling, general and administrative expenses, the analyst says. Also, Social Security Office-registered members at the hospital group fell slightly in 1Q from the prior quarter, driven by a reduction at the group's Kasemrad Hospital Saraburi, the analyst says. The brokerage lowers its 2025-2027 core profit estimates for the group by 7%-11%. It downgrades the stock's rating to hold from buy and lowers the target price to THB16.50 from THB18.50. Shares are 2.0% lower at THB14.90. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Financial Services Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Financial Services. Exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1008 ET - Commerzbank's ambitious targets are aimed at defending it from a unsolicited takeover attempt by Italy's UniCredit, a strategy which seems to be working, AlphaValue's Dieter Hein says in a note. The German bank's first-quarter results came in ahead of analysts' expectations, AlphaValue says. Commerzbank posted its best quarterly net result since 2011 despite booking initial restructuring charges and confirmed its main targets for the year, the analyst says. The bank aims to boost its profitability and shareholder returns to 2028 as part of its defense against UniCredit, AlphaValue says. "This strategy has worked well so far, as Commerzbank's share price has doubled since September 2024." Shares rise 2.6%. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

1004 ET - Commerzbank's capital position gives it room for additional returns to shareholders, Warburg Research's Andreas Plaesier says in a research note. The German bank ended the first quarter with a CET1 ratio--a measure of financial strength--of 15.1%. Commerzbank raised its year-end CET1 guidance to at least 14.5% from 14% and said it aims to apply for its next buyback at the beginning of the third quarter. The revised CET1 guidance provides sufficient flexibility for the capital distribution to shareholders, the analyst says. The timeline is in line with what Warburg expected. Between November and March, Commerzbank completed stock repurchases of 1 billion euros. Shares rise 1.8%. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0945 ET - Commerzbank's net interest income is tracking well ahead of the German bank's updated full-year guidance for the metric, Deutsche Bank's Benjamin Goy and Marlene Eibensteiner say in a research note. The lender reiterated its 2025 guidance across the board, but said it now anticipates a net interest income--the difference between what banks earn on loans and what they pay out on client deposits--of around 7.8 billion euros this year. Its previous guidance stood at 7.7 billion to 7.9 billion euros. Nevertheless, its first-quarter net interest income of 2.07 billion euros beat consensus expectations and, on an annualized basis, it is tracking almost 500 million euros ahead of the bank's guidance, Deutsche Bank says. Shares rise 2%. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0929 ET - U.S. companies placed bonds in the euro credit market in April, attracted by better financing conditions in the euro currency, Matthias Schell, senior credit analyst at LBBW says in a note. U.S. corporate bond issuances contributed to over a third of the monthly volume in the euro credit market, he says. Tech company Alphabet supplied five euro bonds totaling 6.75 billion euros ($7.58 billion) in April, "the largest euro corporate bond transaction in five years." This was followed by the second-largest transaction of the month by U.S. company Visa, consisting of four euro bonds worth 3.5 billion euros, Schell says. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0848 ET - Societe Generale is still expected to improve its profitability and to return surplus capital to shareholders, but these hopes seem to be priced in its shares after a strong rally in the year to date, UBS's Jason Napier and Nicolas O'Sullivan say in a research note. With a CET1 ratio--a measure of financial strength--above its target of 13%, the French bank is expected to begin paying interim dividends and launch a share buyback, UBS says. However, SocGen is the top performer among big European banks' stocks so far this year and UBS cuts its recommendation to neutral from buy, while raising its target price to 48 euros from 44 euros. Shares rise 0.3% to 46.67 euros and are up 72% in the year to date. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0806 ET - Mediobanca's quarterly results are ahead of expectations, but newsflow around possible deals with Monte dei Paschi and Banca Generali remains the key driver for its shares, Barclays's Paola Sabbione and Dibin Meledath Koruthu say in a research note. The Italian bank's net profit for its fiscal third quarter was 8% ahead of Barclays's forecast and 7% ahead of consensus estimates, thanks to better-than-expected revenue and costs that were in line with forecasts, the analysts say. Mediobanca also confirmed its guidance for fiscal 2025. Shares rise 2.4%. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0414 ET - Monte dei Paschi's first-quarter profit and revenue came in better than expected overall, helped by a rise in fees that exceeded forecasts and offset a steeper-than-anticipated fall in net interest income, Equita Sim's Andrea Lisi says in a research note. The Italian bank's revenue benefited from higher contributions from commissions and trading, Equita says. Fees came in 5% above Equita's expectations, with sustained momentum in investment fees, the analyst says. On the minus side, net interest income--the difference between what banks earn on loans and pay on deposits--fell 8% compared with the fourth quarter and missed Equita's forecast by 2%, the analyst says. Shares rise 1.2%. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0317 ET - Commerzbank's first-quarter earnings beat expectations thanks to a better-than-anticipated top-line performance, RBC Capital Markets' Anke Reingen says in a research note. Net interest income--the difference between what banks earn on loans and pay on deposits--in the first quarter was only slightly down compared with the fourth quarter, RBC says. The German bank's guidance for 2025 net interest income implies an average decline in the quarterly rate relative to the first quarter, the analyst says. Meanwhile, Commerzbank's fees picked up from the fourth quarter, driven by strong growth in its securities business, German private and small-business customers segment, and fees related to capital markets, she adds. Commerzbank shares rise 1.2%. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0042 ET - DBS Group should be able to maintain higher return on equity levels, CGS International analysts say as they upgrade the stock to add from hold. The Singapore bank has more than enough capital to sustain its return initiatives for 2025-2027, they write in a note. DBS has maintained quarterly ROE of 15.8%-19.4% since 3Q 2022, they add. Its S$3 billion share buyback program between 2025-2027 could also further lift its long-term ROE by 0.5%, they say. CGS International raises the stock's target price to S$47.90 from S$43.10. Shares are up 1.8% at S$43.85.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0030 ET - ANZ is seen by UBS analyst John Storey as the most interesting name in Australian banking as the lender prepares for a new CEO. Storey says that Nuno Matos will have a clean slate when he starts work next week, calling out costs, capital and provisions as three areas of focus. He says ANZ is expected to change significantly under Matos and adds that investors are wondering whether it can improve shareholder returns. The stock is trading at a steep discount to peers and its own historic ratings, he adds. UBS cuts its target price by 3.2% and stays neutral on the stock, which is down 0.7% at A$29.19. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

0027 ET - ANZ is likely to cut its dividend at the end of its current fiscal year, Macquarie analysts reckon. They tell clients in a note that Nuno Matos is likely to review the Australian bank's capital structure after taking over next week, which would lead to a lower shareholder payout. Macquarie analysts see significant execution risks in ANZ's technology overhaul and integration of its Suncorp Bank acquisition. With soft underlying trends and a lack of visibility on outlook, they see little chance of the stock rerating. Macquarie cuts its target price by 1.8% to A$27.50 and keeps a neutral rating on the stock. Shares are down 0.85% at A$29.15. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

2331 ET - DBS remains a safe-haven asset for investors to hide out and tide through uncertainties as well as market volatility, say RHB Research analysts in a note. The Singapore bank's 1Q results were in line, with management broadly keeping its 2025 guidance, they note. Although the impact from U.S. tariff policies are still uncertain, DBS doesn't think that it would derail its capital returns and dividend commitments, they say. "Its dividend and capital returns policies provide investors with clear visibility as to the dividend yield that can be expected ahead," they add. RHB Research maintains a buy rating on the stock and target price of S$47.00. Shares are up 1.55% at S$43.76.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

data.title: Energy & Utilities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Energy and Utilities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1523 ET - Oil futures rise for the first week in the last three after the market shakes off the scare over OPEC+'s accelerated production plans and turns its focus to U.S. trade talks--including this weekend with China--that could ease demand-loss worries. U.S. benchmark WTI had additional support from the EIA's report of a larger-than-expected stock draw, including at Cushing, Ritterbusch says in a note. "U.S. crude supplies have tightened since Liberation Day largely in response to uncertainty regarding the tariffs, and until the U.S. crude deficit begins to narrow again, WTI will be acquiring background price support," the firm adds. WTI settles up 4.7% on the week at $61.02 barrel, and Brent rises 4.3% to $63.91. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1412 ET - Lower diesel prices thanks to the drop in crude are offering some relief to trucking firms that are seeing financial pressure from higher cost of wages, equipment and insurance, says Matt Muenster, chief economist at transport technology firm Breakthrough. Breakthrough lowered its forecast for wholesale diesel prices over the next 12 months by 6%. "We expect OPEC+ production will limit upward price pressure on crude oil during the summer, and diesel margins will face downward price pressure by the middle of June with refinery utilization ramping up," Muenster says. Strong growth in diesel demand so far this year, supported by front-loading of cargo and inventory builds, is likely to moderate in May and June given tariff-related headwinds, he adds. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1330 ET - The number of rigs drilling for oil in the U.S. fell by five this week to 474, the least since late January and 22 below the year-ago level, oil services company Baker Hughes reports. A number of U.S. oil companies said they are reducing planned capital expenditures because of low oil prices, and the EIA lowered its 2025 production estimate to 13.4 million barrels a day from 13.5 million b/d. "The oil market knows that when WTI is in the $50s, there will be a slowdown, the next question is how fast, how deep," says Neil Crosby of Sparta Commodities. "The market will trade once it sees how quickly this is slowing or even declining, and that will take some weeks or months," he adds. Rigs directed at natural gas are unchanged this week at 101. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

0944 ET - Oil futures are higher and heading for gains in a week that started with a slump on planned OPEC+ output increases and is ending with optimism about U.S. trade talks. "All eyes will be on this weekend's trade negotiations with China which should give more direction on global trade," Dennis Kissler of BOK Financial says in a note. Indonesian plans to buy more U.S. petroleum products to get a trade deal, and added U.S. sanctions against buyers of Iranian oil are supportive of crude, but the conflict between India and Pakistan bears watching, he adds. "While currently it seems contained, an escalation could temporarily hurt oil demand from India." WTI is up 1% at $60.51 a barrel and Brent is up 0.9% at $63.38 a barrel. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

0711 ET - Enel delivered a good start to the year and appears to be on track to deliver full-year earnings at the upper end of its guidance, BofA analysts write. The Rome-based energy company could announce a 1 billion euro share buyback quite soon, they add. Enel will prioritize spending this year on organic growth in grids, with a particular focus on Italy, as well as acquiring cheap brownfield renewable assets in the U.S., the analysts write. Shares trade up 2% at 7.74 euros.(adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

0557 ET - Orsted shares trade at a premium to peers, and with balance sheet concerns and limited comfort on the U.S. outlook, the stock risks falling, Citi's Jenny Ping and Marc Ip Tat Kuen write. "We continue to be concerned about Orsted's balance sheet, with [the] company selling operating cashflows to fund U.S. projects and canceling value-creating projects such as Hornsea 4." Tariffs and the U.S. administration's recent cease-to-develop order on the Empire Wind project increase uncertainty. This uncertainty means a higher risk premium, while the removal of Hornsea 4 and potential tariff-related capex all lead to downside. Citi downgrades Orsted to sell from neutral and lowers its target price to 211 Danish kroner from 300 kroner. Shares rise 0.8% to 252.50 kroner. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

0326 ET - EDP made a strong start to 2025, with its first-quarter results exceeding expectations thanks a solid performance in Portugal and Spain, RBC Capital Markets' Fernando Garcia and Charlotte Mettyear say in a research note. The Portuguese utility reported a recurring Ebitda of 1.43 billion euros that topped both consensus expectations of 1.35 billion euros and RBC's estimate of 1.36 billion euros, the analysts say. The company achieved this result despite the fact that it recorded no gains from asset sales last quarter against a gain of 134 million in last year's comparable period, RBC says. The strong results benefited from high hydropower volumes that are likely to continue at least in the second quarter given that Iberian reservoirs are 20% above average as of May, the analysts say. Shares rise 4.7%. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

data.title: Basic Materials Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Basic Materials. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

2250 ET - Banpu's profit is likely to recover in 2H, UOB Kay Hian analysts say in a report, as the brokerage anticipates a recovery in coal and gas prices. The Thai company has both coal and gas businesses. Coal futures could stay above $110 a ton, and gas prices will probably rebound after 2Q's low season, supported by ongoing declines in U.S. gas inventories, the analysts say. The brokerage maintains the stock's hold rating. However, it trims the stock's target price to THB4.80 from THB5.00 based on slightly lowered coal regional core price-to-earnings mean of 8.1X. Shares closed 4.0% higher at THB4.70 on Thursday. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

2140 ET - Petronas Chemicals' near-term earnings outlook may remain challenged by prolonged downtime at its Kertih and Pengerang plants, CIMB Securities analyst Nurzulaikha Azali says in a note. She expects its Pengerang petrochemicals complex's utilization rate to stay low in 2025 due to feedstock issues, and sees margin pressure from rising input costs and weak demand. While the company has minimal direct exposure to U.S. tariffs, structural issues and global trade risks may delay a meaningful rebound, even as a potential feedstock discount and demand recovery could support 2026 performance, she reckons. The analyst cuts her estimates for Petronas Chemicals' 2025-2027 earnings by 13%-26%. CIMB lowers Petronas Chemicals to hold from buy and trims the target price to MYR3.53 from MYR4.41. Shares are 0.6% higher at MYR3.47. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

1943 ET - The current quarter is a big opportunity for Aeris Resources now that its refinancing is squared away, according to Bell Potter. Analyst David Coates says it's positive that no copper or gold hedging was required by its new loan facility with Washington H. Soul Pattinson. "However, we also estimate that an increase in debt service costs of A$4 million-A$5 million per annum will partially offset the cash release and delayed repayment schedule," Bell Potter says. It sees the current quarter as a chance for Aeris to lift production materially "This would contribute to debt reduction by end 2025 and demonstrate the latent production capacity at Tritton--both key positive catalysts, in our view," Bell Potter says, referring to its copper mine. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

1910 ET [Dow Jones]--Orica, the world's largest maker of industrial explosives, has the balance sheet to support more share buybacks, contends Jefferies. It expects leverage of 1.45x at 1H to move toward the bottom end of Orica's 1.25-2x target range through 2H. "We forecast leverage will moderate further in coming periods, which provides Orica with ample flexibility to top up the existing A$400 million buyback with further capital management initiatives, further underpinning the underlying EPS growth profile," analyst Ramoun Lazar says. Jefferies has a buy call on Orica. (david.winning@wsj.com)

1857 ET [Dow Jones]--Near term, North American lumber markets are expected to remain volatile, Interfor said, but added mid-term, lumber from Canada is expected to remain a key source of supply to meet U.S. needs. Interfor also said near-term volatility could be affected by potential tariffs on Canadian lumber exports to the U.S. Growth in U.S. lumber manufacturing capacity may be limited by labor constraints, lengthy equipment lead times, and other factors, Interfor said, adding it "expects that over the mid-term, lumber markets will continue to benefit from favorable underlying supply and demand fundamentals." (stephen.nakrosis@wsj.com)

data.title: Auto & Transport Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering the Auto and Transport sector. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1412 ET - Lower diesel prices thanks to the drop in crude are offering some relief to trucking firms that are seeing financial pressure from higher cost of wages, equipment and insurance, says Matt Muenster, chief economist at transport technology firm Breakthrough. Breakthrough lowered its forecast for wholesale diesel prices over the next 12 months by 6%. "We expect OPEC+ production will limit upward price pressure on crude oil during the summer, and diesel margins will face downward price pressure by the middle of June with refinery utilization ramping up," Muenster says. Strong growth in diesel demand so far this year, supported by front-loading of cargo and inventory builds, is likely to moderate in May and June given tariff-related headwinds, he adds. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1002 ET - Air Canada's good balance of destinations and offerings has allowed it to take the edge of the worst effects of trade concerns on demand. Citi analyst Stephen Trent says investors have been rightfully focused on the risks to the carrier's cross-border flow, however, Air Canada "continues to have among the strongest weightings of lucrative, international long-haul exposure in the Americas." This is a good buffer to counter risks. "Overall, the results and guidance look positive, with Ebitda and earnings coming above consensus, solid free cash flow generation and guidance adjustments that only mildly softened the 2025 Ebitda range," Trent says. (adriano.marchese@wsj.com)

0855 ET - Air Canada is managing what it can control after navigating turbulence of uncertainty in 1Q. Scotiabank's Konark Gupta says the Canadian flagship airline reported better-than-feared 1Q Ebitda and free cash flow thanks to its strong cost management and bookings as traffic faced headwinds. The analyst notes that management trimmed full-year Ebitda guidance, supporting Scotiabank's estimate at the low-end and consensus forecasts at the high-end. This is due to a reduced capacity target, but the company maintained its adjusted cost per available seat mile and FCF guidance thanks to cost and capex savings. (adriano.marchese@wsj.com)

0808 ET - Daimler Truck could post a first quarter beat on margins, but guidance could be downgraded due to weakness in heavy duty and medium duty markets, Morgan Stanley analysts write. The bank thinks earnings will come in ahead of expectations, with the industrial adjusted EBIT margin at 8.8% versus consensus at 8.5%. It sees upside driven by a strong quarter in Trucks North America. However, Daimler Truck's peers now expect a 2025 North American truck market of 275,000 units, versus 300,000 previously. The bank expects the company to adopt a similar market outlook given recent order weakness. It thinks management may guide to a 7%-9% industrial adjusted EBIT margin, down from 8%-10%. "Consensus estimates have already reduced, meaning downside on the day may be limited." (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

0517 ET - Maersk maintained its earnings guidance, but the upper-end of the range looks optimistic in the current demand backdrop, JPMorgan analysts write. Maersk delivered a solid first quarter result which came in ahead of expectations, driven by better ocean freight rates as volumes were flat. Management revised downwards its market volume forecast, noting that trade uncertainty is paralysing for a lot of customers. The bank models a quarter-on-quarter deterioration in EBIT in the coming quarters, with material losses into 2026 if Red Sea disruption ends, which could drive 13% capacity growth and a 45% decline in spot rates next year. It reiterates its underweight rating and lowers its price target to 7,900 Danish kroner from 7,850 kroner. Shares trade flat at 11,575 kroner. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

0446 ET - International Consolidated Airlines has a stronger outlook than the airline group is given credit for, RBC Capital Markets analyst Ruairi Cullinane says in a research note. The view comes as the company, also known as IAG, said that it is continuing to see good demand for air travel. It posted first-quarter results in which demand for premium seats mitigated some weakness in U.S. economy demand. "We think IAG has a stronger outlook than given credit for, given a supportive capacity backdrop on the Atlantic, pricing potential from BA product measures, and given scope for business travel recovery to step up further," the analyst says. Shares trade up 2.1% at 296.34 pence. (pierre.bertrand@wsj.com)

0445 ET - Uncertainty over the demand for third-quarter transatlantic economy flights may temper consensus upgrades for International Consolidated Airlines as the company's result for the first quarter implies around 6% upgrades for the full year, Jefferies analysts say. "We see value in IAG shares, given further self-help opportunities, relatively small carbon headwinds and strong cash generation," they say in a research note. Jefferies adds that the airline group's margins are now higher than before the pandemic, and they can keep growing as the company's long-haul capacity recovers. Shares trade up 1.4% at 294.30 pence. (pierre.bertrand@wsj.com)

0424 ET - IAG's first-quarter earnings exceeded expectations thanks to lower fuel prices and increased revenue, JPMorgan analysts say in a research note. They add that they don't expect any major changes to consensus. The company's pre-exceptional operating profit came to 198 million euros, up from expectations of 133 million euros. Despite the beat and amid continuing uncertainty, IAG maintained its guidance, adding that strength in the premium cabin is offsetting recent softness in U.S. economy leisure. "This is the first public commentary around any incremental softness on Transatlantic," the analysts say. JPMorgan adds that IAG's second-quarter revenue being ahead of the prior-year level is a positive development. Shares trade up 1.6% at 295 pence. (pierre.bertrand@wsj.com)

0408 ET - International Consolidated Airlines' result shows the airline group is improving by leaps and bounds, Richard Hunter at Interactive Investor says in a research note. The company's results bode well for the rest of the year as its important North Atlantic market showed a 13% increase in passenger unit revenue. "Indeed, investors who chose to buy in to the recovery while the shares were on the tarmac have been handsomely rewarded and the group's improving financial strength and general growth lead to an increasingly improving flight path," Hunter says, adding that market consensus for the shares as a buy is unlikely to waver. Shares trade up 1.1% at 293.6 pence.(pierre.bertrand@wsj.com)

0402 ET - The agreement to reduce tariffs on U.K. car exports into the U.S. is great news for the industry and consumers, U.K. industry body Society of Motor Manufacturers and Traders said. President Trump had applied a 25% tariff on imported cars, but a deal reached Thursday reduces that to 10% for the first 100,000 U.K.-made cars that enter the U.S. "The application of these tariffs was a severe and immediate threat to U.K. automotive exporters so this deal will provide much needed relief," Mike Hawes, SMMT Chief Executive said. Hawes hopes that the deal will lead to broader and deeper cooperation that reduces barriers to trade still further, charting a path to economic growth for both nations. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

0339 ET - IAG's balance sheet looks "investable" again, with high free cashflow generation despite elevated spending, JPMorgan analysts say in a note. IAG's re-investments, while leading to higher costs in the short term, can potentially drive revenue and higher EBIT margins in the medium term, JPM says. Current EBIT consensus expectations look too conservative, with the potential for earnings upside from stronger pricing driven by leisure demand, JPM says. Shares are up 0.8% at 292.50 pence.(anthony.orunagoriainoff@dowjones.com)

0258 ET - Jaguar Land Rover said it welcomes the U.K.-U.S. trade deal as it secures greater certainty for the sector and the communities it supports. "The car industry is vital to the U.K.'s economic prosperity, sustaining 250,000 jobs," Chief Executive Adrian Mardell said in a statement. The British automaker, owned by India's Tata Motors, sells over 400,000 cars annually, with the U.S. accounting for around a quarter of global sales. President Trump and Prime Minister Starmer announced an agreement Thursday that reduces a 25% tariff on all imported cars to 10% for the first 100,000 made in the U.K. There was a blanket 2.5% tariff on U.K. cars exported to the U.S. before President Trump took office. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

data.title: Brown-Forman, Korbel Champagne End Relationship
data.description:
Brown-Forman and Korbel Champagne Cellars reported Friday the conclusion of their sales, marketing and distribution relationship, effective June 30.

"Going forward, we will each pursue our respective long-term growth objectives," said Brown-Forman CEO Lawson Whiting.

data.title: S&P 500 Posts Weekly Decline as Health Care, Communication Services Drop
data.description:
The Standard & Poor's 500 Index fell 0.5% this week as declines led by health care and communication services outweighed gains paced by industrials.

The S&P 500 ended the week at 5,659.91, up 1.6% in May and down 3.8% this year.

Concerns about the Trump administration's tariff policies have weighed on the stock market. Those worries were underscored on Wednesday as Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell, speaking after the central bank's policy-setting committee left interest rates steady, said: "If the large increases in tariffs that have been announced are sustained, they're likely to generate a rise in inflation, a slowdown in economic growth and an increase in unemployment."

A bit of optimism emerged Thursday after a trade deal between the US and the UK was announced. On Friday, President Donald Trump indicated in a social media post that an 80% tariff on Chinese imports, which would reflect a cut from 145%, may be under consideration as Chinese and US officials prepare to meet this weekend. Many analysts still consider 80% as a high tariff rate, and no further deals have been announced.

This week among sectors, health care posted the largest drop, falling 4.3%, followed by a 2.4% decline in communication services. Consumer staples, real estate and materials also declined.

Vertex Pharmaceuticals shares fell 15%. The company reported Q1 adjusted earnings and revenue that trailed estimates by analysts. At least two analysts downgraded their ratings on the stock.

In communication services, Match Group shares shed 11%. The company plans to cut its workforce by 13%.

Among the sector rallies in the week, industrials climbed 1.1%, followed by a 0.8% gain in consumer discretionary, a 0.5% rise in utilities and a 0.4% advance in energy. Technology and financials eked out small increases.

Rockwell Automation shares jumped 16% this week. The company reported fiscal Q2 results that topped Wall Street projections and raised full-year earnings guidance.

Next week's earnings calendar features Cisco Systems (CSCO), Walmart (WMT), Deere (DE) and Applied Materials (AMAT).

Economic data will include the April consumer and producer price indexes, two closely watched inflation readings. April retail sales, industrial production and housing starts, as well as May consumer sentiment, are expected next week.

data.title: ACELYRIN Inc expected to post a loss of 50 cents a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:
• ACELYRIN Inc is expected to show no change in quarterly revenue when it reports results on May 12 (estimated) for the period ending March 31 2025

  • ​LSEG's mean analyst estimate for ACELYRIN Inc is for a loss of 50 cents per share.

data.title: The Stock Market Is Getting a Warning from Bonds and the Dollar. Tariff Turmoil Isn't Over. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Patti Domm

A month out from President Donald Trump's decision to ease up on the tariff throttle, something still isn't quite right in the financial markets. Stock investors should be wary.

Corporate spreads have narrowed, and Treasury yields are less volatile, soothed by positive talk on tariffs. Stocks have recovered from their April 2 "Liberation Day," losses, but the dollar remains under pressure. Gold is the safe haven of choice.

But worries that tariffs and trade disruptions will impact the economy remain, and any sign that hard data is weakening or inflation is picking up could unleash another round of searing volatility. That is most visible in assets other than stocks.

Financial markets convulsed after Trump's "Liberation Day" tariffs on dozens of countries were broader and higher than expected. Stocks fell sharply. Bond yields fell, too, but then rose erratically, while the dollar weakened. The president then paused the tariffs for 90 days on April 9.

President Donald Trump announced on Thursday that the U.K. was the first trading partner to address to a new deal. Although the country has a trade surplus with the U.S., it agreed to a 10% minimum tariff on most exports.

"There's a clear fragility here, because tariffs aren't going back to zero," said Peter Boockvar, chief investment officer at Bleakley Financial Group. The U.K. deal indicates that 10% is the new minimum for tariffs, "which is a much higher tariff rate than we had before April 2," he said.

The S&P 500 has since risen by about 2% above its April 2 close. That "is telling us [investors] think we're going back to a zero-tariff world. The markets are way too 'la la la' that everything is going back to the pre-tariff time frame," Bookvar said.

Trump has pledged more deals soon. Investors are focused on talks between Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and Chinese officials, to be held in Geneva this weekend. On Friday the president suggested Chinese tariffs could be lowered to 80% from the current 145%.

As the tariff developments unfold, there continues to be an unusual decoupling of markets.

The dollar is 7.5% lower on the year against a basket of currencies and was weaker again Friday. Gold has benefited from the greenback's lost luster, adding more than 26% since the start of the year.

"Gold and the dollar are at odds with the macro outlook that is being painted by the equity market," said George Goncalves, MUFG head of U.S. macro strategy. "What if the dollar is telling the true story that there's weakness out there, and U.S. assets aren't going to get the kind of capital they used to?"

The 10-year Treasury yield rose to about 4.3% Thursday, and the 2-year yield to 3.85%.

"If you look at financial conditions, the reason why it's better is all equities," sid Goncalves. "Credit has retraced maybe a third to half of the move, whereas equities have retraced the whole thing."

Bessent and many in the markets said tariffs would likely raise the dollar. Instead, it has moved lower.

"The dollar has only retraced about 20% of the [Liberation Day] decline. If you look at the Nasdaq, it has fully retraced. There is some permanent damage to the dollar in that sense," said Hans Mikkelsen, head of U.S. credit strategy at TD Securities.

The shift away from dollar assets by foreigners and others has also shown up in the corporate debt market, which Mikkelsen said had been too richly priced at the end of last year. It hasn't returned to those levels.

Yields on investment-grade corporate debt rose relative to Treasuries in the immediate aftermath of the April 2 tariffs, indicating investors were concerned that companies' risk of default had grown. But those spreads have since fallen by roughly half, Mikkelsen said. The recent peak was 119 basis points on April 8, right before the pause. The spread is currently 102, compared with about 80 at the end of last year when U.S. assets were in high demand. A basis point is one-hundredth of a percent.

Mikkelsen said he believes the market is pricing in tariffs of about 10% across the board, but he expects the final levies to be higher. Trump needs tariffs to raise new revenue to pay for tax cuts, he said.

"He's going to need to show hundreds of billions of offsetting revenues, " said Mikkelsen, adding tariffs of 25% could generate that. The president's other goal is to rebuild U.S. manufacturing. "It can't be just 10% tariffs because that is not going to drive a lot of companies to set up in the U.S. I think the markets are probably on average expecting 10%, which is one of the reasons I think this is the calm before the storm."

Inflation data expected next week will give investors an important signal of how tariffs may his the stock market going forward. Mark Cabana, head of U.S. rates strategy at Bank of America Investors said he is concerned about stagflation. A bad reading on the consumer price index as some point could prompt the Federal Reserve to openly consider increasing interest rates.

With growth already slowing, "that's a very bad recipe for risk assets," he said.

Write to editors@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Customers Bancorp, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Customers Bancorp, Inc., a financial services company primarily engaged in providing banking services, has released its Form 10-Q report for the first quarter of 2025. The report provides a detailed overview of the company's financial performance and operational highlights, reflecting the challenges and opportunities faced in the current economic environment.

Financial Highlights

Net Interest Income: $167.4 million, increased by $7.1 million primarily due to lower interest expense on deposits.

Provision for Credit Losses: $28.3 million, increased by $11.2 million reflecting a slight deterioration in macroeconomic forecasts and an increase in loan balances.

Total Non-Interest Income (Loss): $(24.5) million, decreased by $45.7 million primarily due to a $51.3 million impairment loss on investment securities.

Total Non-Interest Expense: $102.8 million, increased by $3.6 million mainly due to higher salaries and employee benefits and professional services.

Income Before Income Tax Expense (Benefit): $11.9 million, decreased by $53.5 million due to higher provision for credit losses and impairment loss on investment securities.

Income Tax Expense (Benefit): $(1.0) million, decreased by $16.7 million primarily due to lower pre-tax income and an increase in discrete tax benefits from share-based compensation.

Net Income: $12.9 million, decreased by $36.8 million compared to the prior year.

Net Income Available to Common Shareholders: $9.5 million, decreased by $36.4 million, reflecting the overall decrease in net income.

Preferred Stock Dividends: $3.4 million, decreased by $0.4 million compared to the prior year.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: Customers Bancorp derives the majority of its income from interest on loans, leases, and investments. The primary source of funds for these activities is deposits and borrowings. The net interest margin, a key measure of success, increased slightly due to a favorable shift in deposit mix and lower market interest rates on deposits.

Geographical Performance: The company operates primarily in Berks County and Southeastern Pennsylvania, New York, New Jersey, Massachusetts, Rhode Island, New Hampshire, California, and Nevada. The specialized lending and mortgage finance loans are nationwide, indicating a broad geographical reach.

Sales Units: The commercial equipment financing group focuses on industries such as transportation, construction, marine, and manufacturing, with $705.8 million in equipment finance loans and $269.2 million in equipment finance leases outstanding as of March 31, 2025.

Future Outlook: The Federal Reserve's actions on interest rates and the macroeconomic environment, including inflation and geopolitical conditions, are expected to impact the company's operations. Customers Bancorp is focused on maintaining liquidity, managing credit risk, and optimizing its loan portfolio to navigate these uncertainties.

SEC Filing: Customers Bancorp, Inc. [ CUBI ] - 10-Q - May. 09, 2025

data.title: Creative Media & Community Trust Corp SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Creative Media & Community Trust Corp, a company specializing in the leasing of office and multifamily real estate assets, has released its Form 10-Q report for the first quarter of 2025. The report provides a detailed overview of the company's financial performance and operational activities, highlighting key metrics and future outlooks.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenues: $32.3 million, reflecting a decrease from the previous year's $34.0 million, primarily due to lower rental and other property income.

Net Loss: $(6.3) million, compared to $(3.9) million in the prior year, indicating a larger loss primarily driven by increased interest expenses and general and administrative costs.

Net Loss Attributable to Common Stockholders: $(11.9) million, compared to $(12.3) million in the prior year, showing a slight improvement despite the overall net loss.

Net Loss Attributable to Common Stockholders Per Share: $(20.73), a significant improvement from $(125.46) in the prior year, largely due to an increase in the weighted average shares of common stock outstanding.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments

The company operates as a lessor of both office and multifamily real estate assets, with rental and other property income being a significant revenue segment. For the three months ended March 31, 2025, fixed lease payments contributed $14.4 million, while variable lease payments added $2.9 million.

Geographical Performance

The company has significant investments in Los Angeles, California, including properties at 1910 Sunset Boulevard, 4750 Wilshire Boulevard, 1902 Park Avenue, and 1015 N Mansfield Avenue. These properties are part of joint ventures and contribute to the company's operational footprint in the region.

Sales Units

The company is involved in the development of multifamily units, such as the 36 units at the 1915 Park Project adjacent to the 1910 Sunset Boulevard office building, expected to be completed by the third quarter of 2025.

New Production Launches

The company is evaluating development options for the 1015 N Mansfield Avenue site, which is in the pre-development phase for potential creative office or commercial space.

Future Outlook

The company is focused on completing the 1915 Park Project by the third quarter of 2025 and is exploring development options for the 1015 N Mansfield Avenue site. Additionally, the company is in discussions to restructure the terms of the Channel House Mortgage, which may impact future operations.

SEC Filing: Creative Media & Community Trust Corp [ CMCT ] - 10-Q - May. 09, 2025
